title: Trump Says He Will Order T.S.A. Agents Paid as Funding Deal Stalls
summary: President Trump’s announcement came as talks on Capitol Hill over funding the Department of Homeland Security faltered, and airport lines continued to grow.

title: D.H.S. Funding Lapse Leads to Longest Partial Shutdown in History
summary: If the shutdown continues after this weekend, it will be longer than any previous shutdown, partial or full.

title: Trump Officials Investigate Stanford, Ohio State and U.C. San Diego Medical Schools
summary: The Justice Department’s demands for admissions-related data from Stanford, Ohio State and the University of California, San Diego, reveal an expansion of its higher-education pressure campaign.

title: Trump Eyes White House Treaty Room for Latest Renovation Project
summary: President Trump is making new plans in one of the most significant renovations in the history of the White House.

title: What’s on Trump’s Mind During a Cabinet Meeting? Sharpies, Iran and Inflation.
summary: President Trump waxed on about the virtues of the pen, calling it more economical and a better instrument than the fancier writing tools preferred by his predecessors.

title: U.S. Stocks Have Their Biggest Drop Since Start of Iran War
summary: The S&P 500 tumbled 1.7 percent on Thursday and oil prices continued rising as investors fret about when the war would end.

title: Iran Keeps a Tight Grip on Strait of Hormuz, Pressuring Shipping and Energy Sectors
summary: Iran has allowed a small number of vessels to pass, but that won’t alleviate pressure or risk for the shipping industry and energy markets any time soon.

title: G.O.P. Senator Weighs Forcing Congress to Vote to Authorize the Iran War
summary: Senator Lisa Murkowski of Alaska is drafting a formal authorization for the use of military force in Iran, seeking to put some parameters around the operation as the Trump administration has boxed out Congress.

title: Kennedy’s Vaccine Agenda Hits Roadblocks, Diminishing His Clout
summary: Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.’s vaccine skepticism is posing challenges for the Trump administration. Top health jobs are unfilled, and a court has blocked his vaccine schedule changes.

title: When a Not-So-Dark Knight and His Sidekick Saved a Wacky Gotham
summary: Joel Schumacher apologized for “Batman & Robin,” his corny 1997 superhero movie, but thanks to its ice puns and bat nipples, it’s since become an accidental parody worth howling at.

title: Safety Experts Considered LaGuardia Challenging but Not an Outlier
summary: Regulators, pilots and others in aviation have worried about the kind of runway accident that happened at LaGuardia Airport on Sunday.

title: Air Canada C.E.O. Apologizes for English-Language Condolences After Plane Crash
summary: Michael Rousseau said he was “deeply saddened” that his ​inability to speak French had diverted attention from the families’ grief.

title: Trump Defends His Use of Mail-In Voting: ‘Because I’m President’
summary: President Trump wants to restrict voting by mail, which he says amounts to “cheating.” But he defended his own use of the practice in a special election this week.

title: Sheriff in California Seizes More Ballots, Ignoring State Attorney General
summary: Chad Bianco, the Riverside County sheriff and a Republican running for governor, is examining votes from 2025 after unsubstantiated claims of fraud.

title: Savannah Guthrie Says 2 Ransom Notes About Her Mother Were Likely Genuine
summary: Her interview on the “Today” show came more than 50 days after her mother, Nancy Guthrie, was taken from her home near Tucson, Ariz.

title: Roman Catholic Churches See a Surge of New Converts
summary: Bishops are trying to understand what’s behind the wave. People joining the church described their reasons as highly personal.

title: Saving Hermit Crabs by Breeding Them in the Suburbs
summary: Often treated as throwaway pets, hermit crabs can live 50 years. Mary Akers, a self-taught expert, wants people to appreciate them as much as she does.

title: Local Opposition Is Slowing A.I. Data Centers. Wall Street Has Noticed.
summary: Tech companies are running into resistance from neighbors and may not be able to build at the pace they promised investors.

title: Judge Stays Pentagon’s Labeling of Anthropic as ‘Supply Chain Risk’
summary: The decision is an early victory for the artificial intelligence company in a rancorous legal battle with the Department of Defense.

title: Seeking a Sounding Board? Beware the Eager-to-Please Chatbot.
summary: A new study of popular AI models shows that their feedback on social situations is far from impartial.

title: Wall St. Bonuses Soared to $49.2 Billion. New York Hoped for Even More.
summary: The city was counting on a 15 percent rise in bonuses to produce tax revenues to fill its budget gap. The actual increase was 9 percent, the state comptroller said.

title: How a Healthy Mind-Set Influences Longevity
summary: A few qualities, including a sense of purpose, seem to have real benefits — especially as you age.

title: How Can America Be So Miserable When It’s So Rich?
summary: The just resentments of a shrinking middle class.

title: It Was One of the Cold War’s Greatest Crimes. No One Has Paid a Price.
summary: No one apart from the Congolese people has ever paid a price for the CIA-backed assassination of Patrice Lumumba.

title: Trump Gives Iran More Time to Reopen the Strait of Hormuz
summary: Also, transgender athletes were barred from women’s Olympic events. Here’s the latest at the end of Thursday.

title: California Renames Cesar Chavez Holiday After Sex Abuse Revelations
summary: Gov. Gavin Newsom signed legislation to rename Cesar Chavez Day as “Farmworkers Day,” moving quickly before the March 31 holiday.

title: High School Athlete Is Shot Dead While Playing With Friends
summary: A boy, the son of a New York police officer, may have been playing with a gun when it accidentally went off, killing Ka’Mardre Coleman, 16, according to prosecutors and the boy’s lawyer.

title: Sardi’s Sells Its Name and Takes a Brief Intermission
summary: The longtime owner of the restaurant, a Theater District mainstay, is bowing out, and the Shubert Organization plans to reopen after a renovation, with the celebrity caricatures intact.
